docs: add .env.example template file
prs.body: This PR adds a `.env.example` file based on the environment variables listed in the `README.md`. Adding this example file makes it much easier and faster for new users to scaffold the project and understand the exact format and structure of the expected `env` configuration without having to manually guess or extract it from the README.

prs.title: fix: Add a pointer cursor to all modal buttons
prs.body:
Closes #44

### Description
Currently, the modal buttons in the UI do not show a pointer cursor when hovered over, which fails to provide visual feedback that the elements are clickable.

### Changes Made:
- Added `cursor: pointer;` to the `.modal-btn` class in `elements.css` to improve the overall user experience and give clear visual feedback.

prs.title: feat: Add 'Start Detecting Plant Disease' CTA button in Hero Section
prs.body: Closes #11 ### Description This PR addresses issue #11 by adding a Call-To-Action (CTA) button in the Hero section of the landing page to improve user engagement. ### Changes Made: - Added a `` component wrapping the CTA button below the image grid in `src/landing_page/home/Hero.js`. - The button routes the user to the `/new` page (disease detection form). - Applied Bootstrap styling (`btn-success`, `btn-lg`, `shadow`, `rounded-pill`) to match the platform's green agricultural theme (as seen in `index.css`). - Explicitly added inline hover effects using `onMouseOver` and `onMouseOut` for interactive scaling and shadowing. - Maintained responsive alignment with `.text-center` and margin-top spacing (`.mt-5`) so it avoids overlapping content across devices. ### Screenshot of Changes - Before - After

prs.title: feat: add hover state and transition for `modal-element`
prs.body:
Closes #42

This PR addresses the issue indicating that the `.modal-element` felt static.
- Added a `:hover` state to `.modal-element` to subtly alter its border-color and background, making it feel more interactive.
- Added a `transition` to border-color and background for smooth animation.

prs.title: Add DummyJSON API
prs.body: Adds [DummyJSON](https://dummyjson.com/) to the Test Data section. It's a free fake REST API for placeholder JSON data including products, users, posts, and more. It does not require authentication and supports CORS and HTTPS.

prs.title: [WinGet] feat: add WinGet release date badge
prs.body:
Closes #11285

Adds a new `/winget/release-date/{name}` badge that displays the `ReleaseDate` field from a package's latest version installer manifest.

### How it works

1. Fetches the list of version directories (same GraphQL query as existing version badge).
2. Finds the latest version using the existing `latest()` helper.
3. Fetches the content of `{name}.installer.yaml` via a second GraphQL query.
4. Extracts the `ReleaseDate` YAML field from the manifest text.
5. Renders a formatted date badge using `renderDateBadge()`.

### Edge cases handled

- Package not found: `package not found`
- ReleaseDate field absent (it is optional per WinGet schema): `release date not available`
- Installer manifest missing: `installer manifest not found`

### Tests

Three mocked tests added and passing. Live test requires a GitHub token in `local.yml` (same as the existing WinGet version badge).

prs.title: fix(frontend): update border color to match other borders in External APIs
prs.body:
Closes #9402. The border colors in DomainDetails were using a mix of `var(--bg-slate-500)` and `var(--bg-slate-400)`. This unifies them to `var(--bg-slate-400)` so that the border color matches the other borders consistently, particularly visible in light mode.

<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
---

> [!NOTE]
> **Low Risk**
> Low risk CSS-only change that standardizes border colors; impact is limited to visual styling (especially in light mode) with no logic changes.
> 
> **Overview**
> Updates `DomainDetails.styles.scss` to **standardize borders** across the Domain Details drawer/table views by replacing remaining `var(--bg-slate-500)` border usages with `var(--bg-slate-400)`, making the UI border color consistent with other External APIs surfaces.
> 
> <sup>Written by [Cursor Bugbot](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot) for commit c0e9aaafc49bc480140ad368d14f61e9a168e419. This will update automatically on new commits. Configure [here](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot).</sup>
<!-- /CURSOR_SUMMARY -->

prs.title: fix(sms): support literal \n in SMS OTP templates
prs.body:
Fixes supabase/supabase#6435

## Summary
Users want to use newlines (`\n`) in their SMS OTP templates configured via environment variables, but SMS providers were sending the literal string `\n` instead of an actual newline character.

This PR fixes the issue by replacing any literal `\n` sequences in the SMS template string with actual newline characters before parsing the Go template.

## Changes
- In `internal/conf/configuration.go`, added `strings.ReplaceAll(template, "\\n", "\n")` before parsing both `Sms.Template` and `MFA.Phone.Template`

prs.title: feat(i18n): add Mongolian (mn-MN) translation
prs.body:
Fixes #9880

## Summary
Added Mongolian (mn-MN) locale translations for Excalidraw.

The locale file covers major UI elements including:
- Toolbar labels
- Drawing properties (stroke, fill, opacity, etc.)
- Canvas actions
- Color names
- Toast notifications
- Dialog titles and buttons
- Welcome screen text
- Command palette
- Key bindings

prs.title: fix(ui): prevent Tab key default behavior in arrowhead picker
prs.body:
Fixes #9656

## Summary
When using Tab to cycle through arrowhead options in the arrow type picker panel, the browser was also moving focus to the next focusable element outside the picker because `event.preventDefault()` was not called.

This fix adds `event.preventDefault()` to the Tab key handler in `IconPicker.tsx`, preventing the default focus-shift while still performing the arrowhead selection via Tab cycling.

prs.title: fix: correct memory cleanup index in uv_os_environ error path
prs.body:
## Problem

The `uv_os_environ()` function had a memory cleanup bug in its error handling path. When memory allocation failed during environment variable processing, the cleanup loop was using the wrong index variable to free allocated memory.

## Root Cause

In the `fail:` cleanup block, the code was using `cnt` (the count of successfully allocated items) as the array index instead of `i` (the loop variable). This meant:
- The same memory could be freed multiple times (if `cnt < i`)
- Not all allocated items would be properly freed
- Potential crashes or undefined behavior

## Solution

Changed the cleanup loop to use the correct index variable `i` instead of `cnt`:
- `src/unix/core.c`: Line 1536
- `src/win/util.c`: Line 1283

## Impact

- Prevents double-free errors
- Ensures proper memory cleanup on allocation failure
- Improves reliability of error handling in `uv_os_environ()`

## Testing

The existing test suite (`test/test-env-vars.c`) covers `uv_os_environ()` functionality. This fix ensures proper cleanup when memory allocation fails during environment variable processing.

prs.title: Fix: Add null checks in Raycaster and BufferGeometry to prevent runtime errors
prs.body:
## Description

This PR fixes three bugs related to missing null/undefined checks that could cause runtime errors:

### Changes

1. **Raycaster.intersect()** - Added null/undefined check at the start of the function
   - Prevents `TypeError: Cannot read property 'layers' of null` when null objects are passed
   - Handles edge cases where objects might be null during scene graph traversal

2. **Raycaster.intersectObjects()** - Added null/undefined checks when iterating over objects array
   - Prevents errors when arrays contain null or undefined values
   - Gracefully skips invalid entries instead of throwing exceptions

3. **BufferGeometry.normalizeNormals()** - Added check for normal attribute existence
   - Prevents `TypeError: Cannot read property 'count' of undefined` when called on geometries without normals
   - Provides a helpful warning message when the attribute is missing

### Impact

- **Breaking Changes**: None
- **Bug Fixes**: Prevents runtime errors in edge cases
- **Performance**: Minimal overhead from additional checks

### Testing

The changes maintain backward compatibility and handle edge cases gracefully. No existing functionality is affected.

prs.title: fix(rehype): handle promise rejections in lazy language loading
prs.body:
### Description

Fixes a bug where promise rejections weren't properly handled when lazy-loading languages in the rehype transformer. If `loadLanguage()` failed for any language, the rejection would propagate to `Promise.all()` without proper error handling, potentially causing unhandled promise rejections.

### Changes

- Added `.catch()` handler to promises pushed to the queue when lazy-loading languages
- Errors are now properly delegated to `fallbackLanguage` or `onError` callback when language loading fails
- Prevents unhandled promise rejections in production

### Testing

- No linter errors introduced
- Error handling follows the same pattern as synchronous error handling in the same function

issues.title: Add Website Icon (Favicon) for Better Branding and Visual Identity
issues.body:
## Feature Description

Currently, the website does not have a favicon (tab icon), which makes it appear incomplete in browser tabs and bookmarks.

## Why is this feature needed?

Adding a favicon will help with branding, recognition, and overall polish.

## Additional Context

If needed, I can help design or create a simple icon that matches the project’s theme and colors as per your description.

issues.title: Enhance Front Page Banner
issues.body: ## Feature Description Update the front page banner to use a horizontal scroll layout instead of the current slide based system. This change will create a smoother, more modern scrolling experience while still maintaining the slideshow style feel. ## Why is this feature needed? The current slideshow transitions can feel slightly abrupt. A horizontal scroll implementation with overflow-x styling would provide: - A smoother transition between banners - A more intuitive UX for touch and mouse users - Retention of the “slideshow” look through auto scroll or snapping ## Additional Context

issues.title: Search function isn't working properly
issues.body: The search functionality on the site is not working properly. Currently, it only shows results from the page the user is on, instead of searching across the entire site/database. Steps to Reproduce: Navigate to any page on the site. Use the search bar to look for a keyword that exists on another page. Notice that no results are shown unless the keyword exists on the current page. Expected Behavior: Search should return results from the entire site (all pages/data), not just the current page. Actual Behavior: Search is limited to the currently loaded page, making it unreliable and incomplete. Proposed Solutions: Implement server-side search to query the full database rather than just page content. Please assign me this under Hacktoberfest 2025

issues.title: Enhancement Never Ending in draw
issues.body:
Never-Ending Draw Mode:
In case of a draw, we can implement a “never-ending” Tic Tac Toe mode:

Remove the earliest move from the board. Make that box empty, allowing the players to continue playing indefinitely. This keeps the game engaging even after a draw.

Attachments:
Screenshot showing the current alert-based result announcement.

Assign me this under Hactober fest this will be engaging and will reduce the most unentertaining outcome "Draw".

issues.title: Improve Game Result Handling
issues.body: In the attached screenshot, you can see that the current Tic Tac Toe game announces the result (win/draw) using a browser alert. Improvements Conditional Rendering for Result: Instead of using alert, we can display the game result directly in the UI using conditional rendering. This will make the game flow smoother and less intrusive. Optional: Highlight which part of the code triggers the alert so it can be replaced with the conditional rendering logic.

issues.title: Improve Text Readability in "Key Features" Section
issues.body:
## 📌 Description:
The current text in the "Key Features" section on the upload page has a very low contrast with the background (especially "Key Features", "NotesVault", and the tagline). It appears too light and is hard to read.

## ✅ Proposed Change:
I would like to update the color of this text to a more readable shade of blue (or another high-contrast color that fits the theme). This will improve accessibility and overall user experience.

## 🔧 Page Affected:
[https://opensource-society.github.io/notesvault/pages/upload.html](https://opensource-society.github.io/notesvault/pages/upload.html)

## 🙋‍♂️ Can I work on this?
I’m a GSSoC’25 contributor. Kindly assign this issue to me if approved.
